skill.name: m365-agents-dotnet
skill.description: Guides developers through Microsoft 365 Agents SDK for .NET patterns, including ASP.NET Core hosting, AgentApplication routing, authentication setup, and Copilot Studio client integration.
skill.summary: Build Microsoft 365 and Teams agents with the .NET Agents SDK.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command, filesystem, network, and weak-crypto findings were reviewed in context. The command and network matches are Markdown examples and official Microsoft endpoints, while the weak-crypto and hidden-file matches are false positives; however, the sample error handler can expose exception messages to users if copied directly.

medium_findings.title: Sample Error Handler Exposes Exception Messages
medium_findings.description: The agent sample sends exception.Message back in an end-of-conversation activity. If copied into production, this can disclose internal implementation details or sensitive error text to chat users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly assigns exception.Message to outbound activity text. This is a real disclosure risk in copied sample code, although the skill itself is documentation.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Fenced Examples
low_findings.description: The detected dotnet and C# blocks are Markdown examples for installing packages and building sample agents. They are not executable scripts in the skill package and do not show command injection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside Markdown code fences and no runnable helper script exists in the scanned file. The commands use fixed package names rather than user-controlled input.

low_findings.title: Network URLs Are Expected Microsoft References
low_findings.description: The detected URLs point to Microsoft identity, Bot Framework, localhost development, Microsoft Learn, and the Microsoft agents GitHub repository. No evidence found of data exfiltration or unrelated external endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible, vendor-relevant, and match the stated Microsoft 365 Agents purpose. I did not find hidden callback URLs or suspicious domains.

low_findings.title: Development Endpoint Requires Deployment Care
low_findings.description: The sample only requires authorization outside development mode and binds a local development URL. This is reasonable for local testing, but users must keep authorization enabled for deployed agents.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The condition clearly separates development and non-development behavior. The risk depends on how users adapt the sample for production.

low_findings.title: Weak-Crypto and Hidden-File Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto matches are text-pattern collisions in descriptive or enum-related content, and the hidden-file match is the OAuth scope suffix .default. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA1, or hidden file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not contain cryptographic algorithm calls or file operations. They contain frontmatter text, an OAuth scope string, and EndOfConversationCodes.Error.

content.value_statement: Microsoft 365 agent projects require correct hosting, routing, authentication, and Copilot Studio integration choices. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for .NET agent implementation.
actual_capabilities: Explains package setup for Microsoft.Agents .NET projects.
Shows ASP.NET Core hosting patterns for agent message endpoints.
Describes AgentApplication routing for welcome, message, and error handlers.
Covers MSAL-based authentication settings for Microsoft 365 agents.
Outlines Copilot Studio direct-to-engine client integration.
Lists official Microsoft reference links for API verification.
limitations: Does not include a complete runnable project template.
Requires checking current Microsoft documentation before implementation.
Uses placeholders for tenant, client, secret, and environment values.
Does not cover deployment infrastructure or production monitoring.

use_cases.title: Create a Teams Agent Prototype
use_cases.description: Plan a basic ASP.NET Core agent that receives Teams messages and routes them through AgentApplication handlers.

use_cases.title: Review Agent Authentication
use_cases.description: Check whether a .NET agent configuration includes token validation, audience settings, and Microsoft identity authority values.

use_cases.title: Connect to Copilot Studio
use_cases.description: Draft the client-side integration steps for calling a Copilot Studio agent through the Microsoft Agents SDK.

prompt_templates.title: Start a New Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline the first files and packages for a Microsoft 365 Agents SDK .NET prototype.

prompt_templates.title: Add Message Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design AgentApplication handlers for welcome messages, user messages, and turn errors.

prompt_templates.title: Review Authentication Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Microsoft 365 agent authentication configuration and list missing production safeguards.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Copilot Studio Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a Copilot Studio direct-to-engine client flow with MSAL token acquisition and HttpClientFactory.

output_examples.input: Plan a Microsoft 365 agent prototype for Teams.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation checklist covering packages, ASP.NET Core hosting, the message endpoint, AgentApplication routing, and local testing notes.

output_examples.input: Review my production readiness for an agent endpoint.
output: Token validation and authorization checks to verify.
Storage and secret handling changes needed before deployment.
Error handling changes to avoid exposing internal messages.

output_examples.input: Explain how to connect a .NET app to Copilot Studio.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step integration plan covering client settings, MSAL token acquisition, HttpClientFactory usage, and conversation startup.

best_practices: Verify current Microsoft Agents SDK APIs before generating final code.
Keep secrets in managed configuration providers, not committed settings files.
Require authorization and token validation for every deployed message endpoint.
anti_patterns: Do not copy development-only localhost settings into production deployments.
Do not expose raw exception messages to chat users.
Do not rely on MemoryStorage for production conversation state.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build Microsoft 365, Teams, and Copilot Studio agents using the Microsoft Agents SDK for .NET.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete application?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance, patterns, and review prompts rather than a full project scaffold.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is prepared for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require Microsoft documentation checks?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs users to verify the latest Microsoft Agents SDK APIs and NuGet package versions.

faq.question: Can it help with authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers token validation, MSAL configuration concepts, and Microsoft identity authority settings.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium warning for secure copy-paste use of the sample error handling and development endpoint patterns.
